items.myth: You need a third-party library to build a CLI.
items.reality: argparse in the standard library handles positional/optional args, types, choices, subcommands and help; libraries like Click/Typer are conveniences, not requirements.
items.example: A 30-line `argparse` setup with subcommands does everything most CLIs need: `--help`, type conversion, error messages, exit codes. Reach for Click/Typer for ergonomics, not capability.

items.myth: argparse values are already the right type.
items.reality: Everything is a string unless you set type=; numbers and other types must be declared to be converted and validated.
items.example: Without `type=int`, `--limit 10` arrives as `'10'`, and `args.limit + 1` raises `TypeError: can only concatenate str ...`. Always declare types.

items.reality: argparse auto-generates -h/--help and a usage string from your argument definitions and help texts.
items.example: Set `description=` on the parser and `help=` on each `add_argument`; running `mycli --help` shows a polished usage screen — no extra code.
